Metadata.title: Soy Niña - Empowering Girls and Adolescents in Costa Rica
Metadata.description: Costa Rican NGO dedicated to the holistic development of girls, adolescents, and families in situations of social vulnerability through socio-educational programs.

Hero.description: Socio-educational programs transforming lives in San José, Heredia, and Alajuela — building futures from the inside out.
Hero.imageAlt: Girls learning together in Costa Rica

Programs.description: Four complementary programs that support girls, adolescents, and their families — from elementary school through early adulthood.

program1.title: Club Niña
program1.description: Develops socio-emotional skills, critical thinking, and self-care in elementary school girls.

program2.title: Club Cole
program2.description: Empowers adolescents by strengthening their autonomy, decision-making, and life planning.

program3.title: Club Familias
program3.description: Holistic support to strengthen family bonds, parenting skills, and community development.

program4.title: Tutoring
program4.description: Personalized educational reinforcement to improve academic performance and prevent dropout.

Impact.description: Since 2018 we have worked in the most vulnerable communities of Costa Rica's Greater Metropolitan Area. These numbers represent real lives, strengthened families, and a more just future.

stat1.label: Lives transformed
stat1.detail: Since 2018, more than 600 girls, adolescents, and their families have participated in our programs across 5 communities in the Greater Metropolitan Area.

stat2.label: Active girls today
stat2.detail: Currently more than 200 girls and adolescents actively participate in our 4 weekly programs: Club Niña, Club Cole, Club Familias, and Tutoring.

stat3.label: Volunteer hours
stat3.detail: Our network of more than 200 professional volunteers has contributed 28,000 hours of support in social work, psychology, education, and mentoring.

stat4.label: School retention
stat4.detail: 95% of program participants stay in the education system, compared to significantly lower national averages in at-risk areas.

area1.title: Self-esteem and leadership
area1.description: Girls develop self-confidence, learn to use their voice, and become agents of change in their communities.

area2.title: Violence prevention
area2.description: Education in rights, healthy boundaries, and support networks that protect girls and adolescents from risky situations.

area3.title: Academic success
area3.description: Personalized tutoring and mentoring that transform girls' relationship with education and open opportunities.

t1.quote: I used to not like going to school. Now I know I can be whatever I want.
t1.author: María, age 11

t2.quote: It changed the way I see education. Watching the girls progress is transformative.
t2.author: Eugenia

t3.quote: My daughter now stands up for her rights. That's priceless.
t3.author: Carolina

Impact.ctaTitle: Want to be part of the change?
Impact.ctaDescription: Join as a volunteer or donate so more girls can access our programs.

Events.description: Workshops, festivals, and sessions where our girls, families, and volunteers come together to grow.

Donate.description: Every contribution, no matter the size, makes a difference. 85% of our funds go directly to programs — we publish annual financial reports.

l1.title: Friend
l1.description: Materials for one girl for a month

l2.title: Ally
l2.description: Snacks for 10 girls for a week

l4.title: Sponsor
l4.description: Full program support for one month

Volunteer.description: Volunteers are the heart of Soy Niña. If you believe in the power of education to transform lives, we have a place for you — from facilitating clubs to creating digital content.

r6.title: Donors

Volunteer.testimonialQuote: "I found a community of women who believe in education with equity."

QuienesSomos.metaDescription: Meet our team, board of directors, corporate allies, and university partners at Soy Niña.
QuienesSomos.heroSubtitle: Meet the team driving change at Soy Niña
QuienesSomos.historyP1: In February 2018, the conviction was born to accompany girls and adolescents in contexts of psychosocial vulnerability. We started in Los Guido, Desamparados, working from the perspective of respectful education and human rights.
QuienesSomos.historyP2: We expanded to Río Azul in 2019 and reached Pavas in 2021. In each community, we work in contexts marked by urban violence, early pregnancies, drug trafficking, and inequality. Our purpose is to provide safe spaces where girls can be supported in their holistic development.
QuienesSomos.historyP3: Today, we continue growing. In 2025, we will expand to new communities, always faithful to our mission of educating with excellence, equity, and sisterhood.
QuienesSomos.universitySubtitle: Universities supporting our training and internships
QuienesSomos.ctaDescription: Join our volunteer team or become a corporate ally.

ProgramasPage.metaDescription: Discover Soy Niña's programs: Club Niña, Club Cole, Club Familias, and Academic Tutoring.
ProgramasPage.heroSubtitle: Spaces for transformation and holistic growth
ProgramasPage.methodologyP1: We offer free, continuous programming during the school year with professional facilitators in Social Sciences and Education. Each club has 2-3 trained volunteers who accompany the facilitators.
ProgramasPage.methodologyP2: We include special sessions in STEAM, health, environment, and leadership. Everything is done from the perspective of respectful education and human rights, prioritizing socio-emotional well-being.
ProgramasPage.ctaDescription: All our programs are completely free for beneficiaries.

EventosPage.calendarDescription: Explore our upcoming events. From leadership workshops to community festivals, each activity is an opportunity to grow, learn, and connect. Select a month to see scheduled events.
EventosPage.ctaDescription: Join as a volunteer or bring your family to our activities. We look forward to seeing you!

VoluntariadoPage.metaDescription: Join as a national, corporate, or international volunteer, or do a professional internship with Soy Niña.
VoluntariadoPage.heroSubtitle: Be part of the change we want to see
VoluntariadoPage.whyDescription: At Soy Niña, volunteers are the heart of the program. If you believe in the power of education to transform lives, we have a place for you. Our volunteers contribute from different spaces: facilitating clubs, teaching, supporting families, recruiting resources, or sharing their talents in communications.
